channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
item.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

item.title: Apple Maps may be about to get ads
item.description: Apple Maps may soon serve ads in top results when users search on terms like "restaurants."

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Is Building an AI CEO. Will He Lay Himself Off?
item.description: After the metaverse fiasco, maybe it would be for the best.

item.title: The Next PlayStation May Rely on AI More Than Any Console Before Before It
item.description: Get ready for "fake frames" on the PlayStation 6, or whatever Sony is cooking up next.

item.title: The Ending of ‘Project Hail Mary’ Teased a Surprising Offscreen Subplot
item.description: Ryan Gosling stars in Phil Lord and Chris Miller's adaptation of Andy Weir's novel, in theaters now.

item.title: Polymarket adopts new stance to get tougher on insider trading
item.description: The prediction market added new rules that govern bets on outcomes based on insider information. Polymarket is updating the rules of its platform to crack down on insider trading as the prediction market giant looks to curb scrutiny over market manipulation.

item.title: The kid-friendly Fitbit Ace is $100, which matches its best price
item.description: If you’re trying to figure out how to keep your kids occupied this spring break, the Fitbit Ace LTE is an easy way to get them outdoors and off their phones. The kid-friendly smartwatch encourages movement with trackable games, and it’s back to an all-time low price of $99.95 ($80 off) at Amazon ahead of […]

item.title: 'We're supposed to start prepping on it soon': The Mummy 4 gets its first tease from Ready or Not 2 directors — it's going to be chaotic
item.description: The Mummy 4 is officially slated to start filming in August — and Ready or Not 2 directors already make it sound absolutely unhinged.

item.title: I switched back to earbuds after using headphones for three years — here’s what I have and haven’t missed
item.description: From travel convenience to overall aesthetics, here are three things I've missed with using earbuds — and three things I haven't.

item.title: Samsung has smart glasses landing later this year — here are 4 details leaks have already revealed, including displays and XR apps
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Glasses, as leaks are calling them, should land later this year. Here's what we know.

item.title: Claude Code and Cowork can now use your computer
item.description: Anthropic announced today that its Claude Code and Claude Cowork tools are being updated to accomplish tasks using your computer. The latest update will see these AI resources become capable of opening files, using the browser and running dev tools. When enabled, the Claude AI chatbot will first prioritize connectors to supported services such as the Google workplace suite or Slack, but if a connector isn't available, it will be able to still execute an assigned task. Claude should ask for permission before taking these actions, but Anthropic still recommended not using this feature to handle ...

item.title: Meta Unfairly Targeted Older Workers During Layoffs Last Year, Lawsuit Claims
item.description: A laid-off senior director is suing the tech company for wrongful termination and age discrimination.

item.title: Fallen Meteorite May Have Hit a Home in Houston
item.description: NASA confirmed a meteor traveled over Texas after residents spotted a bright fireball in the skies on Saturday.

item.title: Someone has publicly leaked an exploit kit that can hack millions of iPhones
item.description: Leaked "DarkSword" exploits published to GitHub allow hackers and cybercriminals to target iPhone users running old versions of iOS with spyware, according to cybersecurity researchers.
